data.title: Market Chatter: South Korea to Expand Trade Task Force After US Tariff Pause
data.description:
South Korea will broaden its response task force into a full interagency team to address changing US tariff policies, Yonhap News Agency reported Thursday.

The move follows Washington's 90-day suspension of newly imposed reciprocal tariffs, just hours after they took effect, according to the report.

The expanded task force will include eight sector-specific working groups covering areas such as macroeconomy, finance, industry, and SMEs, the report said.

The group aims to strengthen analysis and coordination across agencies and explore longer-term trade strategies, according to the report.

Disclaimer

Market Chatter news is derived from conversations with market professionals globally. This information is believed to be from reliable sources but may include rumor and speculation. Accuracy is not guaranteed.

data.title: Casa Holdings Flags Net Loss for Six Months Ended March 31
data.description:
Casa Holdings is expected to book a net loss for the six months ended March 31, according to a Thursday filing with the Singapore Exchange.

The company has attributed this to foreign exchange losses and the appreciation of the Malaysia ringgit against the Singapore dollar.

The company will release its financial results on or before May 15.

data.title: Alpina Holdings Receives Demand Letter Over Deferred Payment
data.description:
Alpina Holdings received a letter of demand from warrantors regarding a deferred payment of around SG$1.8 million, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The warrantors disagree with the company's view that it is not obliged to make the deferred payment, prompting them to seek arbitration proceedings.

This development comes after the company agreed to acquire the Wan Dormitory for SG$24.5 million and completed the purchase in March.

The company had withheld a deferred payment of SG$2 million from the consideration at completion. The payment was slated to be released within ten business days after fulfilling all deal conditions and completing the SLA tender assignment.

data.title: China's SME Performance Rises in Q1
data.description:
The performance of Chinese small and medium-sized enterprises improved in the first quarter of the year, Xinhua reported Thursday, citing data from the China Association of Small and Medium Enterprises.

The Small and Medium Enterprises Development Index rose to 89.5 from 89 in the previous quarter as consumption during the holidays bolstered, the report said.

The sub-indices for industry, wholesale and retail, transport, social services, and information transmission software all expanded quarter on quarter. Only the construction sub-index remained flat, the report said.

Meanwhile, real estate, accommodation, and catering sub-indices kept on declining, the report said.

data.title: African Markets - Factors to watch on April 11
data.description:
The following company announcements, scheduled economic indicators, debt and currency market moves and political events may affect African markets on Friday.

- - - - -

GLOBAL MARKETS

Global stocks slumped and the dollar sank further on Friday, while a manic bond selloff took hold in a brutal end to the week of tit-for-tat worldwide tariffs that have fed fears of a deep recession and shaken investor confidence in U.S. assets.

WORLD OIL PRICES

Oil prices fell on Friday and were set to drop for a second week on concerns prolonged trade war between the United States and China, the world's largest economies, will crush crude consumption as their dispute curtails economic growth.

EMERGING MARKETS

For the top emerging markets news, double click on

AFRICA STOCKS

For the latest news on African stocks, click on

AFRICA MARKETS

The Nigerian and Ghanaian currencies are expected to be broadly stable in the next week to Thursday thanks to central bank support, while the Ugandan and Zambian currencies could fall, traders said.

SOUTH AFRICA MARKETS

South Africa's rand stumbled on Thursday on global market jitters while stocks jumped, as investors digested U.S. President Donald Trump's announcement of an immediate 90-day tariff pause for many countries.

KENYA MARKETS

Kenya's shilling gained ground against the dollar on Thursday, after U.S. President Donald Trump's unexpected decision to temporarily suspend most of his recently imposed tariffs, traders said.

SUDAN GENOCIDE

Sudan told the International Court of Justice on Thursday that the United Arab Emirates was violating the Genocide Convention by supporting paramilitary forces in Darfur, but the UAE argued the case should be thrown out as the court lacked jurisdiction.

US AID

President Donald Trump's administration wants the U.N. World Food Programme to modify operations backed by the U.S. in six countries, a State Department spokesperson said on Wednesday, after Washington terminated and then reinstated the assistance.

TANZANIA POLITICS

Tanzanian opposition party leader Tundu Lissu was on Thursday charged with treason, a capital offence, for comments he made last week that prosecutors said called on the public to launch a rebellion and disrupt an election due this year.

data.title: U.K. Shopper Traffic Drops in March on Late Easter, Report Says
data.description:
By Michael Susin

U.K. retail footfall fell in March, dragged by a late Easter and ongoing subdued consumer confidence, according to a report published Friday.

The number of visits to stores--comprising main-street shops, retail parks and shopping centers--for the five weeks ended April 5 decreased by 5.4% from the same period a year earlier, according to the report by the British Retail Consortium and Sensormatic Solutions IQ. This compares with the 0.2% increase in February.

The figure, however, represents an on-year distortion given that Easter this year is in April, while in 2024 it was in March. This results in an artificially higher footfall in April, but a lower figure in March.

Footfall at retail parks saw the smallest drop, down 1.2% in March, compared with a 2.0% rise in February. The segment is managing to hold up better than other segments thanks to the offer of hospitality and leisure alongside retail, BRC Chief Executive Helen Dickinson said.

Shopping-center visits decreased by 5.8%, compared with a 0.1% rise the prior month, and main-street stores footfall fell 4.0%, a slight increase of 0.1% from the month prior.

Footfall also decreased across all nations, with Northern Ireland seeing the largest drop, at 9.0%, while England had the smallest decrease of 4.9%.

Looking ahead, global uncertainties regarding tariffs and a potential economic slowdown could further erode consumer sentiment in the coming months, Dickinson added.

Write to Michael Susin at michael.susin@wsj.com

data.title: NEXT Biometrics Q1 Update
data.description:
NEXT Biometrics, reports NOK 7 million in booked revenue in Q1 2025 far belowforecast. The shortfall is due to multiple reasons. The most important is thetemporary stop in the Aadhaar program in India, as previously communicated.

NEXT and a few other competitors were re-certified as expected, but approvalofNEXT sensors was delayed until early March. Even though this situation iscreating clear long-term advantages for NEXT, with improved market share duetofewer competitors, normal business did not resume before the end of thequarter.This affected not only our Indian customers but also some of our customersbasedin China who target the Indian market with their end products.

In addition to the above, NEXT has withheld several deliveries in Q1 to somecustomers to strengthen the commercial terms. There is every reason to believethis is a long-term viable strategy.

NEXT has a robust long-term sales pipeline exceeding NOK 400 million of whichNOK 69 million are planned deliveries in Q2/Q3 combined, providing solidvisibility into the two upcoming quarters with gross margins above 50%.Severalhigh-potential deals are in advanced stages and expected to close in the nearterm.

The company will publish its annual report today, further detailing andexplaining the company's outlook. NEXT will publish the Q1 report on May 14th.

This information in this stock exchange announcement is considered to beinsideinformation pursuant to the EU Market Abuse Regulation and is published inaccordance with section 5-12 the Norwegian Securities Trading Act.

For more information, please contactUlf Ritsvall, CEO, NEXTulf.ritsvall@nextbiometrics.com

Eirik Underthun, CFO, NEXTeirik.underthun@nextbiometrics.com

About NEXT BiometricsNEXT provides advanced fingerprint sensor technology that deliversuncompromisedsecurity and accuracy for the best possible user experience in the payment andfintech, government ID, access control and office and notebook markets. Thecompany's patented NEXT Active Thermal ® principle allows the development oflarge, high-quality fingerprint sensors with superior security level. NEXTBiometrics Group ASA is headquartered in Oslo, with sales, support, anddevelopment operations in Seattle, Taipei, Tokyo, New Delhi and Shanghai.

This stock exchange announcement was published by Eirik Underthun, CFO at NEXTBiometrics Group ASA, on 11 April 2025, at 06:30 hours (CEST).

data.title: All eyes stay on the bond market in final trading day of the week
data.description:
Despite a decent 30-year bond auction yesterday, yields are continuing to surge as broader market dislocations are persisting. 30-year yields in the US shot to a high of 4.95% earlier but is still up around 3 bps near 4.90% currently. That follows from the jump overnight in erasing the decline from late Wednesday, when Trump announced a pause on tariffs. On the week itself, 30-year yields are poised for their largest jump since 1982.

US 30-year Treasury yields (%) weekly chart

There are obviously a lot of moving parts in play as to why Treasuries are being sold off heavily. But even if the auction didn't say so, the main worry is that there is still some form of funding stress in the market. Leveraged funds coming under big pressure is not the sort of thing that breeds confidence, particularly in this fragile market state.

And that is one thing to be wary about in case there are any leaks as to who is feeling the squeeze in all this and if they are able to ride out the turbulence. Otherwise, it is a cascading effect that will see things get much worse before they get better.

But as yields are continuing to surge higher, the ball goes back over to Trump's side of the court.

As we know, it was the bond market - not the stock market - that got him to relent on his reciprocal tariffs position earlier this week.

And yet, Treasury yields are still surging as the market continues to kick and scream. So, what next?

We're essentially playing a game of chicken where someone between Trump, China, or the Fed has to blink first.

Considering their response to all this, you wouldn't want to bet on China to be the first to give in. That leaves only a battle between Trump and the Fed.

At this point in time, it's not an easy call. If Powell & co. do step in with emergency purchases of Treasuries, it will provide some short-term relief. However, the message that it sends is that it just enables Trump to stay on his tariffs crusade for longer. As for Trump backing down further, that's probably the best case scenario for markets - especially if he picks up the phone to call up Beijing.

That's pretty much the situation that leveraged funds have gotten themselves into at the moment. That they have to wait and see how this game of chicken plays out.

data.title: Coforge, Wipro, Infosys lead Nifty IT rebound as Trump's tariff pause eases business jitters
data.description:
Shares of IT companies surged on April 11 after President Trump paused his tariffs on trading partners for 90 days, easing concerns over a potential US recession and possible delays in clients' IT budgets, with reports suggesting India could look at this window to seal a partial trade deal with Trump.

The rise in the stock prices pushed the Nifty IT index higher by nearly 1.3 percent in early trade.

Coforge shares were the top gainer on the index in the morning, rising nearly 4 percent to trade at Rs 6,571 apiece. LTI MidnTree, Mphasis and Wipro shares followed, trading over 2 percent each. Infosys, HCL Tech and Persistent shares meanwhile gained nearly 2 percent each.

TCS shares however bucked the trend to trade in the red with marginal losses at Rs 3,215 apiece. The fall in the stock price comes after the company posted a net profit of Rs 12,224 crore in Q4 FY25, missing estimate. This marked a fall of nearly 2 percent from the net profit reported in Q4 FY24.

The IT sector has been one of the worst-hit in this global market rout as these companies derive major portions of their revenue from the American market. The Nifty IT index crashed over 10 percent between April 2 and April 9.

However, Trump announced the temporary pause on tariffs on April 9, providing much needed relief to Wall Street, and also eased recession fears, with Goldman Sachs rescinding its recession prediction, a move that is seen as a positive cue for the IT sector.

Trump had unveiled a slew of reciprocal tariffs on April 2, with India being charged 26 percent tariff on its US exports. This sent global markets to a spin, with Indian stock markets being no exception. Apart from a global economic slowdown, the tariffs also increased concerns over a possible US recession. Goldman Sachs had earlier predicted 65 percent chance of the US going into a recession in the next 12 months.Disclaimer: The views and investment tips expressed by experts on Moneycontrol.com are their own and not those of the website or its management. Moneycontrol.com advises users to check with certified experts before taking any investment decisions.​​​

data.title: Thakral's Associate Company to Develop Lifestyle Resort in Queensland, Australia
data.description:
Thakral's associated company, GemLife received an approval to develop an over-50s lifestyle resort in Queensland, Australia for AU$428 million, according to a filing with the Singapore Exchange on Thursday.

The project spans 26 hectares and will include 404 homes with facilities worth around AU$20 million.

Shares of the property developer were up nearly 3% in recent trading.

data.title: Wall Street Journal - April 11
data.description:
The following are the top stories in the Wall Street Journal. Reuters has not verified these stories and does not vouch for their accuracy.

- More large law firms including Latham & Watkins, Simpson Thacher, Kirkland & Ellis are in talks with the White House to reach agreements to avert restrictions on their businesses by promising to do pro bono work that President Donald Trump supports, according to people familiar with the matter.

- Mike Jeffries, the former longtime chief executive of Abercrombie & Fitch is suffering from dementia and is unfit to stand trial for alleged sex crimes, according to a court filing released on Thursday.

- The Trump administration is working to force Columbia University into a consent decree that would legally bind the school to follow federal guidelines in how it combats antisemitism, an extraordinary step that could significantly escalate the pressure on the school as it battles for federal funding.

- Scotts Miracle-Gro is moving to separate its cannabis holdings from its core business, at least until the drug moves closer to federal legalization.

- A tourist helicopter plummeted upside down into New York City's Hudson River on Thursday killing all six people on board, including a Spanish family with three children and the pilot, Mayor Eric Adams said.

- Modelo Especial owner Constellation Brands' beer sales are taking a hit as demand from Hispanic populations in key U.S. states slows down amid Trump's crackdown on immigration, company executives said on Thursday.

data.title: Chinese Shares Turn Higher; Tariff Developments in FocusMarket Talk
data.description: Chinese shares are higher at the midday break after a choppy morning session. Investors continue to monitor trade developments as the global trade war becomes a bilateral one between the U.S. and China. The White House clarified overnight that the tariffs imposed on China by President Trump in his second term add up to 145%, not 125%. Semiconductor and auto stocks lead the gains. SMIC rises 2.8% and Hygon Information Technology is 5.4% higher. BYD climbs 1.7% and SAIC Motor adds 1.1%. The benchmark Shanghai Composite Index edges 0.1% higher to 3227.65, the Shenzhen Composite Index gains 0.3% and the ChiNext Price Index climbs 0.5%. (sherry.qin@wsj.com)

data.title: Malaysia Industrial Output at Over 1-Year Low
data.description:
Industrial production in Malaysia grew by 1.5% year-on-year in February 2025, slowing from a 2.1% gain in the previous month.

This marked the weakest expansion in industrial activity since the decline in December 2023, as output decreased at a faster pace for mining and quarrying (-8.9% vs -3.1% in January) and electricity (-2.8% vs -0.2%).

Meanwhile, output accelerated for manufacturing (4.8% vs 3.7%), mainly due to higher output in manufacture of computers, electronic, and optical products (8.4%), manufacture of vegetable and animal oils and fats (17.7%) and manufacture of chemicals and chemical products (5.7%).

On a seasonally adjusted monthly basis, industrial production fell 0.3% in February 2025, reversing a 0.2% gain in the previous month.

data.title: Malaysia Retail Sales Growth Eases to 5.9%
data.description:
Retail sales in Malaysia rose 5.9% year-on-year in February 2025, easing from an 8.2% increase in the previous month.

The slowdown was driven by moderated growth across most subcomponents, particularly in non-specialised stores (5.9% vs 9.7% in January), food, beverages and tobacco (6.4% vs 8.8%), and automotive fuel (6.2% vs 7.8%).

Trade also grew at a softer pace for other household equipment (4.6% vs 4.7%), cultural and recreational goods (8.6% vs 9.4%), other goods (5.5% vs 8.3%), as well as trade via stalls and markets (7.6% vs 10.8%) and sales not in stalls or markets (9.2% vs 9.6%).

On a monthly basis, retail sales declined by 1.5%, the biggest drop since January 2024, following a 0.5% increase in the prior period.

Meanwhile, wholesale trade growth picked up to 5.1% from 4.6%, while motor vehicle trade rose 1.5% year-on-year.

data.title: Pryme to Raise EUR7 Million in Private Placement
data.description:
Pryme plans to raise 7 million euros in gross proceeds through a share subscription agreement with certain investors.

The clean technology company said Thursday it will issue 5,655,652 new ordinary shares in a privately negotiated private placement.

Pryme intends to use the net proceeds to ramp up and optimize its first plant and meet working capital and general corprorate needs.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Haven Assets Gain on Tariff Worries
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

Germany CPI, balance of payments; U.K. GDP, trade, industrial production; no major corporate trading updates expected

Opening Call:

European stock futures rose early Friday. Asian stocks tracked U.S. indexes lower; the dollar fell; yields on longer-duration Treasurys rose; oil futures fell; while gold pushed higher.

Equities:

Stock futures point to a slightly higher open in Europe on Friday. Market cheer on Wall Street over President Trump's tariff pause gave way to fears over an escalating trade war between the world's two largest economies.

The White House said Thursday that the tariffs imposed on China by Trump in his second term add up to 145%, not the 125% it previously indicated.

"Turbulence remains in play," said Matt Simpson, market analyst at Forex.com and City Index. "The excitement of a tariff pause has passed," he added.

Forex:

Haven assets including the Swiss franc strengthened amid continued concerns about the Trump administration's tariff policies.

"The trouble is, this Trump administration has conflicting objectives and challenging horizon mismatch (between geoeconomic ambitions and foreseeable economic/market outcomes)," Mizuho Securities (Singapore)'s Vishnu Varathan said.

"The complexity of myriad moving parts and the idiosyncrasies of this administration warn of a minefield of volatility flares," he said. As such, the "primal instinct to look for cover, underpinning gold, CHF and JPY is understandable," Varathan added.

Bonds:

Following a benign U.S. CPI report showing the core measure falling. Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent said he saw nothing unusual in the markets, thereby disregarding a 13-basis-point rise in the 30-year yield to 4.87% and a 9-basis-point rise in the 10-year yield to 4.42%, providing additional examples of discomfort and dislocation in a highly irritated bond market, said Tony Sycamore, analyst at IG Markets.

Market conditions have deteriorated further early Friday, with the yield on the U.S. 30-year opening 6 basis points higher at 4.93%, edging back toward the precipice of 5%, he added. The yield on the U.S. 10-year is trading 5 basis points higher at 4.48%, not far from the 4.51% high hit on Wednesday, Sycamore noted.

Energy:

Oil fell early Friday amid jittery markets. Trump's 90-day pause on steep "reciprocal" tariffs for most countries doesn't alleviate uncertainty, ANZ said.

"There is skepticism about the outcome of trade negotiations, and that will continue to weigh on investment and thus the growth outlook," ANZ said. Oil prices are coming under pressure amid ongoing worries over a global economic slowdown, it added.

Metals:

Gold rose in Asia amid safe-haven demand spurred by fears of U.S.-China trade war. Tensions between the world's two largest economies could overshadow any signs of de-escalation, GivTrade's Hassan Fawaz said.

Also, the cooler U.S. March inflation reading released overnight may support further gains in gold as it could boost prospects for more Fed rate cuts, he added.

-

Iron ore prices fell in Asia, weighed by uncertainties surrounding U.S. tariffs. Global supply levels have fallen slightly while demand remains solid, Everbright Futures said. Current inventories have also fallen, while short-term prices are significantly weighed by macroeconomic developments, it said, adding that more near-term volatility is expected.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Market Rout Shatters Long-Held Beliefs on Investing

President Trump's tariff mania is rewriting the investing playbook.

Everyday Americans and Wall Street pros alike have long been accustomed to a steady ascent in U.S. stocks. Wading in to buy declines always seemed to pay off, often almost immediately. Moments of crises were met with a vigorous response from the U.S. government, ready to unleash stimulus and step in to calm markets.

The U.S. and China Are Going to Economic War-and Everyone Will Suffer

The gloves are off. The next chapter of U.S.-China decoupling has begun. The pain will be felt everywhere.

In jacking up his tariffs on China-and pausing steep duties on dozens of other nations-President Trump is pushing the world's two biggest economic powers into a battle that will leave neither unscathed and risks tanking the global economy.

Trump Team Races to Cut Piecemeal Tariff Deals With More Than 70 Countries

WASHINGTON-The Trump administration has embarked on a high-speed effort to negotiate ad hoc deals with more than 70 countries hoping to escape higher tariffs, with any agreements likely to fall short of the kind of fully-fledged trade pacts that traditionally shape global trade.

People with knowledge of the discussions say that many of the offers President Trump is touting are preliminary calls or offers from other nations to negotiate, not fleshed-out economic proposals. While the administration has issued broad requirements for the countries coming to the table-such as reducing their tariffs and buying more American goods-the White House was still devising its negotiation strategy Thursday, as the countdown to the July 8 deadline began.

One Corner of Europe Feels Sharp Chill in Trans-Atlantic Ties

KAISERSLAUTERN, Germany-Driving toward the German village of Ramstein-Miesenbach feels like entering a twilight zone. It is no longer Germany, but it isn't quite America either.

The density of pickup trucks and baseball caps increases. Bilingual signs are everywhere and live country music is played every Thursday at Big Emma's brewery.

As Trump Squares Off With Iran, the Middle East Is on Edge

ABU DHABI-The Trump administration's high-pressure campaign to deal with Iran's nuclear program has put U.S. allies in the Middle East on edge that failure at the negotiating table could spark another war.

President Trump has said he prefers a diplomatic solution to stop Iran from acquiring a nuclear weapon, but he has threatened that Iran is "going to be in great danger" if talks don't go well.

Shein's Bargain-App Formula Crumbles Under Trump

The meteoric popularity among American shoppers of China-founded app Shein was greatly helped by duty-free shipping of its ultracheap fashion. After President Trump closed that option for Chinese goods, its clothes will now bear the full impact of his new tariffs.

The U.S. tariffs imposed on Wednesday and China's retaliation throw a wet blanket over all goods trade between the two countries. For Shein, the additional impact of Trump's move to end the so-called de minimis exemption for China means a double hit and perhaps the most pivotal challenge for the fashion giant, whose links to China have long landed it at the center of U.S.-China tensions.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

data.title: Aussie Broadband Bulls Confident on 2028 Target DeliveryMarket Talk
data.description: Aussie Broadband's bulls at Wilsons see the Australian broadband provider's 2028 targets as achievable, and even beatable. Analysts Ross Barrows and Lachlan Woods tells clients in a note that their forecasts largely reflect the company's newly announced goals, which include surpassing A$1.6 billion in annual revenue and recording an Ebitda margin higher than 12.5%. The pair expect to see deeper market penetration through things like increasing services per customer, increased automation and technology investment, and market-share gains at the expense of larger incumbents. Wilsons raises its target price 1.6% to A$5.16 and stays overweight on the stock, which is up 1.3% at A$3.89. (stuart.condie@wsj.com)

data.title: Fitch Upgrades Vallourec's Rating on Stronger Balance Sheet
data.description:
Fitch upgraded the long-term issuer default rating of Vallourec to BBB- from BB+, with a stable outlook, citing improvements in the company's balance sheet.

The rating agency said Thursday the French tubular products manufacturer achieved zero net debt in 2024, and it expects the leverage to remain at the same level on the back of the management's commitment to a conservative financial policy.

It noted that the company is more resilient against the increased uncertainties in the global markets due to its improved credit profile.

data.title: Boss Energy's Third Quarter Sales volume, Pricing Beat Estimates, Euroz Hartleys Says
data.description:
Boss Energy's total uranium sales for the third quarter ended March 31 reached 268,000 pounds at an average price of $84 per pound, beating estimates of 218,000 pounds at $77 per pound, according to a Thursday note by Euroz Hartleys.

On Thursday, Euroz Hartleys said the production of 295,819 pounds of triuranium octoxide during the quarter was broadly in line with both company guidance and its estimate of 295,000 pounds.

The brokerage added that commissioning issues in the drying and packing circuit, flagged during a recent site visit, are being resolved.

Euroz noted that uranium tenor levels remained elevated at 108 milligrams per liter (mg/l) of triuranium octoxide for the March quarter, compared to 116 mg/l of triuranium octoxide in the December 2024 quarter.

The firm maintained Boss Energy's buy rating and its price target of AU$4.50.

data.title: India's Sensex Up 1.9%, Led by Auto, Industrial StocksMarket Talk
data.description: Indian shares are higher in early trade as the U.S. pause on so-called reciprocal tariffs on Wednesday triggers a relief rally. Indian markets were closed Thursday for a public holiday. Auto and industrial stocks are leading the gains. Jaguar Land Rover-owner Tata Motors is up 4.0% and Larsen & Toubro is 2.8% higher. Meanwhile, Tata Consultancy Services is down 0.6% after its fourth-quarter net profit dropped 1.7% on year and missing analysts' estimates. Investors are focusing on earnings and any developments related to U.S. tariffs. The Sensex is up 1.9% at 75232.36. (kosaku.narioka@wsj.com; @kosakunarioka)

data.title: Konecranes Starts Crane Installation at Vietnamese Manufacturing Facility
data.description:
Konecranes commenced the installation of 14 cranes at a new manufacturing facility in Hung Yen, Vietnam, ordered by engineering company Makino in August 2024.

The Finnish crane manufacturer said Thursday the process is anticipated to be completed by the end of April.

data.title: PRESSR: Visa bridges passion for innovation, fintech and football at GITEX Africa 2025
data.description:
Cairo, Egypt – Visa, a global leader in digital payments, is proud to announce its participation as the Financial Innovation Partner at GITEX Africa 2025, set to take place from April 14 to 16 in Marrakech, Morocco. This premier event will spotlight the transformative power of technology and investment opportunities across the African continent.

Visa's presence at GITEX Africa underscores its steadfast commitment to fostering financial inclusion, driving digital innovation, and enabling a cashless economy in the continent and builds on earlier announcements on Visa’s investment to advance digital transformation in Africa by 2027. Through strategic collaborations with governments, startups, and businesses, Visa is playing a pivotal role in accelerating Africa’s digital transformation and economic development.

Visa is the exclusive digital payment provider of TotalEnergies CAF Africa Cup of Nations tournament, Morocco 2025 (AFCON), and the culmination of sport and technology is top of mind in Morocco as the host nation. With this backdrop, Visa is presenting the “Visa Connected Stadium” experience at GITEX Africa, an immersive journey designed to show how digital payments will enhance the experience of travelers and football fans. Visa will showcase how seamless and secure transactions at every touchpoint - from ticket booking, transit, retail and stadium entry will allow fans and visitors to experience frictionless and secure payment ecosystems. Visa will also offer fans a unique opportunity to view the official AFCON and WAFCON trophies on the first day of GITEX Africa (14 April), underlying Visa's commitment to connect African football’s most passionate and engaged audiences through unique and unforgettable experiences.

Visa will also be culminating its third cohort of the prestigious Visa Accelerator Program, where 18 pan-African fintechs will pitch their businesses and innovative solutions at the GITEX 10X Stage on April 15 to an audience of venture capitalists, Visa senior leadership and key ecosystem enablers. These early-stage startups have recently completed 12 weeks of programming, giving them access to 1:1 mentorship, personalized training, and opportunities to receive access to funding, development, and other growth resources. GITEX Africa participants can attend the Demo Day taking place at 14:15pm on the 10X Stage in Hall 23.

Senior Visa executives including Andrew Torre, Regional President, CEMEA; Leila Serhan, Senior Vice President for North Africa, Levant and Pakistan (NALP); Sami Romdhane, Country Manager, Visa Morocco and Godfrey Sullivan, Senior Vice President and Head of Strategy, Products, Solutions, and Digital Partnerships, CEMEA will be sharing exclusive insights on a series of panels over the course of the event.

As GITEX Africa 2025 promises to be an electrifying event, Visa invites attendees to visit its booth at Stand No 16B-20 in Hall 16 to explore cutting-edge payment innovations, engage in thought-provoking discussions, and experience firsthand how digital payments are shaping the future of Africa’s economy.

About Visa Inc

Visa is a world leader in digital payments, facilitating transactions between consumers, merchants, financial institutions and government entities across more than 200 countries and territories. Our mission is to connect the world through the most innovative, convenient, reliable and secure payments network, enabling individuals, businesses and economies to thrive. We believe that economies that include everyone everywhere, uplift everyone everywhere and see access as foundational to the future of money movement. Learn more at Visa.com.
